title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins Best Director Oscar for 'One Battle After Another': 'You Make A Guy Work Hard for One of These'
text: Director Paul Thomas Anderson received the Academy Award for Best Director at the 98th Annual Oscars ceremony for his film "One Battle After Another." This marks his first Oscar win after 11 previous nominations throughout his career. In his acceptance speech, Anderson expressed gratitude and acknowledged the difficulty of winning the award. He stated: "You make a guy work hard for one of these" and dedicated the recognition to his late producer and assistant director, Adam Somner, with whom he collaborated for nearly two decades. "One Battle After Another" received 13 Oscar nominations overall, including Best Picture and acting nominations for Leonardo DiCaprio, Benicio del Toro, Sean Penn, and Teyana Taylor. Anderson also won Oscars for Best Picture (as producer) and Best Adapted Screenplay at the same ceremony.
